response: Both Lancaster Cathedral and Peel Cathedral belong to the Catholic religion. Lancaster Cathedral is a Roman Catholic cathedral in England, serving as the parish church of the Archbishop of Wellington and part of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Lancaster. Peel Cathedral, located on the Isle of Man, is also a Roman Catholic cathedral, serving as the cathedral of the Diocese of Sodor and Man, which is part of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Shillong. Both cathedrals are affiliated with the Catholic Church and function as places of worship within their respective dioceses.
